LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. - THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. LEAVE TO AMEND IS ALLOWED TO STATE ONE OR MORE CLAIMS BASED ON AN ALLEGATION THAT THERE WAS A DIMINISHED PURCHASE PRICE OBTAINED BY KBM AS A RESULT OF THE WRONGFUL CONDUCT BY KNOLL. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT KEEPS ALL THE LANGUAGE IN THE COURT'S RULING EXCEPT FOR ANYTHING THAT IS INCONSISTENET WITH WHAT THE COURT SAID ORALLY IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY TIMOTHY J. DAVIS
Off Calendar Mar-18-2019 Continued To Apr-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-09-2018 Set For Trial On Jul-15-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. (Added Per Ex Parte Order Of 10/22/18)
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Richard Munzinger, Esq. 415-421-6500 of Shartsis Friese LLP for Plaintiff.
Meryl Macklin, Esq. 415-268-1981 of Bryan Cave Leighton Paisner for Defendants Knoll and Jennyfer Aguilera.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court discloses he has worked with Atty. Macklin a long time ago however, it does not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial. The Court shares his views re: tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues other basis. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

The Court modifies the tentative ruling. The Motion is Granted with leave to Amend. Leave to amend is allowed to state one or more claims based on an allegation that there was a diminished purchase price obtained by KBM as a result of the wrongful conduct by Knoll.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that keeps all the language in the Court's ruling except for anything that is inconsistenet with what the Court said orally in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Defendant Jennyfer Aguilera, Knoll, Inc.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Jennyfer Aguilera, Knoll, Inc.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings -
CCP 439 requires counsel to meet and confer before a motion for judgment on the pleadings is filed and to so state in a declaration and there is no such declaration in the register of actions, so this motion is ordered OFF CALENDAR. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru
